title: Devkund Waterfall
tagline: Devkund Waterfall — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Devkund Waterfall is a waterfall located near Bhira, in Raigad district, Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Devkund Waterfall is a waterfall located near Bhira, in Raigad district, Maharashtra, India. It is a 'plunge' waterfall pouring massive amounts of water on the rocky surface underneath. It is a popular spot for one day picnics.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Navlakh Umbre
topPlaces.description: Navlakh Umbre is a village and gram panchayat in Mawal taluka, Pune district, Maharashtra, India. It is located about 100 km from Mumbai in the Western Ghats.

topPlaces.name: Bhira Hydroelectric Project
topPlaces.description: The Bhira Hydroelectric Project is an electricity-generating complex in Bhira, Maharashtra state, India. It generates power using water from the nearby Mulshi Dam.

topPlaces.name: Tamhini Ghat
topPlaces.description: Tamhini Ghat is a mountain passage located between Mulshi and Tamhini in Maharashtra, India. Situated on the crest of the Western Ghat mountain ranges, Tamhini Ghat is noted for its surroundings comprising scenic waterfalls, lakes and dense woods.

topPlaces.name: Adgaon, Mulshi
topPlaces.description: Adgaon is a village in Mulshi taluka of Pune District in the state of Maharashtra, India. The village is administrated by a Sarpanch who is an elected representative of the village as per constitution of India and Panchayati raj (India).

topPlaces.name: Akurdi, Mawal
topPlaces.description: Akurdi is a village in Mawal taluka, in Pune district, in the state of Maharashtra, India.It encompasses an area of 387 ha (956 acres).

topPlaces.name: Nenavali Caves
topPlaces.description: Nenavali Caves, also Khadsamble Caves, are located at Sudhagad in Raigad, India.

topPlaces.name: Dongargaon, Mawal
topPlaces.description: Dongargaon is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 328.47 ha (812 acres).

topPlaces.name: Mulshi Dam
topPlaces.description: Mulshi is the name of a major dam on the Mula River in India. It is located in the Mulshi taluka administrative division of the Pune district of Maharashtra State.

items.note: Navlakh Umbre is a village and gram panchayat in Mawal taluka, Pune district, Maharashtra, India. It is located about 100 km from Mumbai in the Western Ghats.
The Bhira Hydroelectric Project is an electricity-generating complex in Bhira, Maharashtra state, India. It generates power using water from the nearby Mulshi Dam.

Tamhini Ghat is a mountain passage located between Mulshi and Tamhini in Maharashtra, India. Situated on the crest of the Western Ghat mountain ranges, Tamhini Ghat is noted for its surroundings comprising scenic waterfalls, lakes and dense woods.

Adgaon is a village in Mulshi taluka of Pune District in the state of Maharashtra, India. The village is administrated by a Sarpanch who is an elected representative of the village as per constitution of India and Panchayati raj (India).
Akurdi is a village in Mawal taluka, in Pune district, in the state of Maharashtra, India.It encompasses an area of 387 ha (956 acres).
Nenavali Caves, also Khadsamble Caves, are located at Sudhagad in Raigad, India.
Dongargaon is a village and gram panchayat in India, situated in Mawal taluka of Pune district in the state of Maharashtra. It encompasses an area of 328.47 ha (812 acres).
Mulshi is the name of a major dam on the Mula River in India. It is located in the Mulshi taluka administrative division of the Pune district of Maharashtra State.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Devkund Waterfall?
faq.a: The best time to visit Devkund Waterfall is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Devkund Waterfall?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (55 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (49 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Devkund Waterfall?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Devkund Waterfall at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Devkund Waterfall?
faq.a: Stays in Devkund Waterfall start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Devkund Waterfall?
faq.a: The top attractions are Navlakh Umbre, Bhira Hydroelectric Project, Tamhini Ghat, Adgaon, Mulshi, Akurdi, Mawal.
